2570	  epimorphisms. Combined with the automatic preservation of finite limits, the
2571	  short-exact-sequence criterion
2572	  \(\operatorname{Functor.preservesFiniteColimits\_iff\_forall\_exact\_map\_and\_epi}\)
2573	  shows it preserves finite colimits, hence (being also left exact) it has
2574	  \(\operatorname{PreservesHomology}\) by
2575	  \(\operatorname{Functor.preservesHomologyOfExact}\). A homology-preserving functor
2576	  sends quasi-isomorphisms to quasi-isomorphisms degreewise
2577	  (\(\operatorname{quasiIsoAt\_map\_of\_preservesHomology}\)), which is the second
2578	  statement.

2640	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-homology-complex} we have that $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$ is
2641	% quasi-isomorphic to $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}[0]$. Hence by
2642	% the exactness of Hom into $\mathcal{I}$ mentioned above we see
2643	% that $\check{H}^i(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0$ for all
2644	% $i > 0$."
2645	\begin{proof}
2646	  \uses{def:cech_complex, def:section_cech_complex, lem:cech_complex_hom_identification, lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso, lem:injective_of_adjoint, lem:mod_pmod_adjunction, lem:cech_complex_op_identification, lem:section_cech_complex_mapop_iso, lem:hom_into_injective_exact}
2647	  The argument has two independent parts.
2648	
2649	  \emph{Part 1: an injective sheaf is injective as a presheaf.} Sheafification is
2650	  left adjoint to the inclusion
2651	  \(\iota : \mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \hookrightarrow \mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
2652	  of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules into presheaves of
2653	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), and its left
2654	  adjoint sheafification is exact, hence preserves monomorphisms. By
2655	  Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} the right adjoint \(\iota\) carries injective
2656	  objects to injective objects; thus an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
2657	  \(\mathcal{I}\) is injective as an object of \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\). In
2658	  particular the contravariant functor \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-,
2659	  \mathcal{I})\) is exact on \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
2660	
2661	  \emph{Part 2: vanishing.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso} the free
2662	  presheaf complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of
2663	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} is a resolution of
2664	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) concentrated in degree \(0\); equivalently the
2665	  augmented complex \(\cdots \to K(\mathcal{U})_1 \to K(\mathcal{U})_0 \to
2666	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} \to 0\) is exact in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
2667	  Applying the exact functor \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-, \mathcal{I})\)
2668	  of Part 1 to this exact augmented complex yields an exact complex; by the
2669	  identification \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet,
2670	  \mathcal{I}) = \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I})\) of
2671	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification} (with the section {\v C}ech complex
2672	  of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex}), this is precisely the statement that
2673	  the section {\v C}ech complex of \(\mathcal{I}\) is exact in positive degrees, with
2674	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}, \mathcal{I}) =
2675	  \mathcal{I}(U)\) supplying degree \(0\).
2676	
2677	  \emph{Categorical assembly.} Concretely the exactness is produced by the
2678	  opposite-transport bridge. The augmentation \(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}
2679	  \mathcal{U}\) is a quasi-isomorphism (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso});
2680	  taking its opposite via the \(\operatorname{HomologicalComplex.op}\) machinery and
2681	  applying \(\operatorname{preadditiveYoneda}.\mathrm{obj}\,\mathcal{I}\) keeps it a
2682	  quasi-isomorphism, since \(\mathcal{I}\) is injective as a presheaf of modules
2683	  (Part 1) and \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) then preserves homology
2684	  (\(\operatorname{quasiIso\_map\_preadditiveYoneda\_of\_injective}\),
2685	  Lemma~\ref{lem:hom_into_injective_exact}). Transporting source and target of this
2686	  mapped-opposite quasi-isomorphism across the opposite-transport isos
2687	  \(\operatorname{homCechComplexMapOpIso}\)
2688	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_op_identification}) and
2689	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechComplexMapOpIso}\)
2690	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_complex_mapop_iso}) identifies it with the augmentation
2691	  of the section {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
2692	  \mathcal{I})\) onto the mapped image of the degree-\(0\)-concentrated target
2693	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\). The latter has vanishing homology in positive
2694	  degrees, so the section {\v C}ech complex does too. Hence
2695	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\) and
2696	  \(\check{H}^0(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = \mathcal{I}(U)\). This direct route uses
2697	  neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
2698	  \(\delta\)-functor identification.
2699	\end{proof}

2731	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
2732	  \texttt{lemma-ses-cech-h1}.}
2733	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and let
2734	  \[
2735	    0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{H} \to 0
2736	  \]
2737	  be a short exact sequence of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Let \(U \subset X\) be
2738	  open. If there is a cofinal system of open coverings \(\mathcal{U}\) of \(U\) with
2739	  \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\), then the map on sections
2740	  \(\mathcal{G}(U) \to \mathcal{H}(U)\) is surjective. Equivalently, the short exact
2741	  sequence is exact on sections over such \(U\).

2745	% covering

2748	% $s|_{U_i}$ is the image of a section $s_i \in \mathcal{G}(U_i)$.

2752	% Consider the sections
2753	% $$

2755	% $$
2756	% Since $s_i$ lifts $s$ we see that $s_{i_0i_1} \in \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0i_1})$.
2757	% By the vanishing of $\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$ we can
2758	% find sections $t_i \in \mathcal{F}(U_i)$ such that

2760	% s_{i_0i_1} = t_{i_1}|_{U_{i_0i_1}} - t_{i_0}|_{U_{i_0i_1}}.
2761	% $$
2762	% Then clearly the sections $s_i - t_i$ satisfy the sheaf condition

2764	% Hence we win."
2765	\begin{proof}
2766	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
2767	  Let \(s \in \mathcal{H}(U)\). Choose an open covering \(\mathcal{U} : U =
2768	  \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) fine enough that (a) \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U},
2769	  \mathcal{F}) = 0\) and (b) each restriction \(s|_{U_i}\) lifts to a section
2770	  \(s_i \in \mathcal{G}(U_i)\); both can be arranged simultaneously because the
2771	  coverings with property (a) are cofinal and (b) holds on any sufficiently fine
2772	  cover (surjectivity of \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{H}\) is a local condition).
2773	  On the double overlaps form
2774	  \[
2775	    s_{i_0 i_1} = s_{i_1}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}} - s_{i_0}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}}.
2776	  \]
2777	  Because the \(s_i\) all lift the single section \(s\), the difference
2778	  \(s_{i_0 i_1}\) maps to \(0\) in \(\mathcal{H}\), hence lies in the subsheaf
2779	  \(\mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 i_1})\); the family \((s_{i_0 i_1})\) is a {\v C}ech
2780	  \(1\)-cocycle for \(\mathcal{F}\). By \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\)
2781	  it is a coboundary: there are \(t_i \in \mathcal{F}(U_i)\) with
2782	  \(s_{i_0 i_1} = t_{i_1}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}} - t_{i_0}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}}\). Then the
2783	  corrected sections \(s_i - t_i \in \mathcal{G}(U_i)\) agree on overlaps, so by the
2784	  sheaf condition they glue to a global section of \(\mathcal{G}\) over \(U\) whose
2785	  image in \(\mathcal{H}\) is \(s\). Hence \(\mathcal{G}(U) \to \mathcal{H}(U)\) is
2786	  surjective.
2787	\end{proof}

2789	\section{Absolute sheaf cohomology as Ext of the corepresenting object}
2790	
2791	The two vanishing lemmas below speak about the \emph{absolute} sheaf cohomology
2792	\(H^p(U, \mathcal{F})\) of an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module over an open \(U\). Their
2793	dimension-shift proof needs three structural facts about \(H^p(U, -)\): a degree-zero
2794	identification with global sections, vanishing on injective modules, and a long exact
2795	sequence attached to a short exact sequence of modules. We realize \(H^p(U, -)\) as the
2796	\emph{Ext of a corepresenting object} in the single abelian category
2797	\(X.\mathrm{Modules} = \mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\): there is an
2798	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U\) corepresenting the
2799	sections-over-\(U\) functor \(\mathcal{F} \mapsto \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F})\), and we set
2800	\(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = \operatorname{Ext}^p_{\mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}(j_!\mathcal{O}_U,
2801	\mathcal{F})\). The corepresenting object is built \emph{without} the extension-by-zero
2802	functor — it is the sheafification of the free module on the representable presheaf
Let me continue reading the chapter to get to the new scaffold blocks for L1, L2, L3, L4, and the final `lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis`.
Let me continue reading to the critical new scaffold blocks.

2803	\(\mathbf{y}\,U\) — and the realization keeps the injective module in the \emph{second}
2804	Ext argument, where Mathlib supplies all three facts off the shelf with \emph{no} appeal
2805	to restriction of module sheaves. The corepresenting object and its corepresentability
2806	are fixed first, then the following definition fixes the realization, and the Mathlib
2807	dependency anchors after it pin the exact results it rests on.
2808	
2809	\begin{definition}
2810	
2811	\leanok
2812	[Extension-by-zero structure sheaf as a corepresenting object]
2813	  \label{def:jshriek_ou}
2814	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.jShriekOU}
2815	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, lem:mod_pmod_adjunction,
2816	    lem:cech_complex_hom_identification}
2817	  \textit{Corepresenting object for sections over an open, built without the
2818	  extension-by-zero functor.}
2819	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(U \subseteq X\) an open subscheme, regarded through the
2820	  representable presheaf \(\mathbf{y}\,U\) on the site of opens of \(X\). The
2821	  \emph{extension-by-zero structure sheaf} \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U\) is the object of
2822	  \(X.\mathrm{Modules} = \mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) obtained by sheafifying the free
2823	  presheaf of modules on \(\mathbf{y}\,U\):
2824	  \[
2825	    j_!\mathcal{O}_U \;:=\; \operatorname{sheafification}\bigl(
2826	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U)\bigr)
2827	    \;\in\; X.\mathrm{Modules},
2828	  \]
2829	  where \(\operatorname{free}\) is the free presheaf-of-modules functor on the
2830	  representable (the degree-zero generator of the free presheaf complex of
2831	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}) and
2832	  \(\operatorname{sheafification} \dashv \iota\) is the sheafification adjunction of
2833	  Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}. Up to isomorphism this is the classical
2834	  extension-by-zero \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U\) of the structure sheaf of \(U\) along the open
2835	  immersion \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\); crucially, we build only this \emph{object} as a
2836	  sheafified free module, never the extension-by-zero \emph{functor} \(j_!\). The object
2837	  exists unconditionally: the free presheaf of modules on a representable and the
2838	  sheafification of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules are both supplied by Mathlib.
2839	\end{definition}
2840	
2841	\begin{lemma}
2842	
2843	[Corepresentability of global sections over \(U\)]
2844	  \label{lem:jshriek_corepr}
2845	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.jShriekOU_homEquiv, AlgebraicGeometry.sheafificationHomAddEquiv}
2846	  \uses{def:jshriek_ou, lem:mod_pmod_adjunction, lem:cech_complex_hom_identification}
2847	  \textit{The object \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U\) corepresents the sections-over-\(U\) functor.}
2848	  For every \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) there is a natural additive
2849	  isomorphism
2850	  \[
2851	    \operatorname{Hom}_{X.\mathrm{Modules}}\bigl(j_!\mathcal{O}_U,\, \mathcal{F}\bigr)
2852	    \;\cong\;
2853	    \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F}) \;=\; \mathcal{F}(U),
2854	  \]
2855	  natural in \(\mathcal{F}\). Thus \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U\) corepresents the global-sections
2856	  functor \(\mathcal{F} \mapsto \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F})\) on \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\).
2857	\end{lemma}
2858	
2859	\begin{proof}
2860	  \uses{def:jshriek_ou, lem:mod_pmod_adjunction, lem:cech_complex_hom_identification}
2861	  Compose two natural additive isomorphisms. The sheafification adjunction
2862	  \(\operatorname{sheafification} \dashv \iota\) of
2863	  Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction} transports corepresentability through
2864	  sheafification:
2865	  \[
2866	    \operatorname{Hom}_{X.\mathrm{Modules}}\bigl(
2867	      \operatorname{sheafification}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U)),\, \mathcal{F}
2868	    \bigr)
2869	    \;\cong\;
2870	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}\bigl(
2871	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U),\, \iota\mathcal{F}\bigr).
2872	  \]
2873	  The free–Yoneda additive equivalence \(\operatorname{freeYonedaHomAddEquiv}\) of
2874	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification} then identifies the right-hand
2875	  presheaf Hom-group with the evaluation \((\iota\mathcal{F})(U) = \mathcal{F}(U) =
2876	  \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F})\):
2877	  \[
2878	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}\bigl(
2879	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U),\, \iota\mathcal{F}\bigr)
2880	    \;\cong\;
2881	    \mathcal{F}(U).
2882	  \]
2883	  Both isomorphisms are additive and natural in \(\mathcal{F}\), so their composite is
2884	  the asserted natural additive isomorphism.
2885	\end{proof}

2887	\begin{definition}
2888	
2889	\leanok
2890	[Absolute sheaf cohomology as Ext of the corepresenting object]
2891	  \label{def:absolute_cohomology}
2892	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.absoluteCohomology}
2893	  \uses{def:jshriek_ou, lem:jshriek_corepr, lem:ext_bifunctor_mathlib,
2894	    lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib, lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib,
2895	    lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib, lem:hasext_standard_mathlib}
2896	  \textit{Realization of absolute sheaf cohomology in the module-sheaf category.}
2897	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\mathcal{F}\) an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(U \subseteq
2898	  X\) an open subscheme with open immersion \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\). Let
2899	  \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U \in X.\mathrm{Modules}\) be the corepresenting object of
2900	  Definition~\ref{def:jshriek_ou}. The \emph{absolute sheaf cohomology of \(\mathcal{F}\)
2901	  over \(U\)} is defined to be the Ext of this corepresenting object in the single
2902	  abelian category \(X.\mathrm{Modules} = \mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of sheaves of
2903	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules:
2904	  \[
2905	    H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) \;:=\; \operatorname{Ext}^p_{\mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}
2906	      \bigl(j_!\mathcal{O}_U,\; \mathcal{F}\bigr),
2907	    \qquad p \geq 0.
2908	  \]
2909	  Both Ext arguments live in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\); no restriction of \(\mathcal{F}\)
2910	  along \(j\) is taken. Here \(\operatorname{Ext}\) is Mathlib's Ext bifunctor
2911	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_bifunctor_mathlib}) on \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\); the
2912	  \(\mathrm{HasExt}\) structure it requires is available unconditionally by
2913	  Lemma~\ref{lem:hasext_standard_mathlib}. The three structural facts the downstream
2914	  dimension-shift arguments consume are then exactly the corresponding Mathlib results:
2915	  \begin{enumerate}
2916	  \item \textbf{Degree zero is global sections.} In degree \(0\), Ext is the Hom-group,
2917	    \(\operatorname{Ext}^0(j_!\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F}) \cong
2918	    \operatorname{Hom}_{X.\mathrm{Modules}}(j_!\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F})\), by the
2919	    degree-zero Ext comparison (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib}); composing
2920	    with the corepresentability isomorphism
2921	    \(\operatorname{Hom}_{X.\mathrm{Modules}}(j_!\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F}) \cong
2922	    \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F})\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:jshriek_corepr} gives \(H^0(U,
2923	    \mathcal{F}) \cong \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F})\).
2924	  \item \textbf{Injective vanishing.} When the \emph{second} Ext argument is an
2925	    injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{I}\), one has
2926	    \(\operatorname{Ext}^{n+1}(-, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) by
2927	    Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib}. Since \(\mathcal{I}\) is exactly
2928	    the second argument of \(\operatorname{Ext}^{n+1}(j_!\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{I})\),
2929	    this yields \(H^{n+1}(U, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) directly: the short exact sequence stays
2930	    in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\), no restriction is taken, and
2931	    restriction-preserves-injectives is not needed.
2932	  \item \textbf{Long exact sequence.} For a short exact sequence \(0 \to \mathcal{F}
2933	    \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\) and the
2934	    \emph{fixed} first argument \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U\), the covariant Ext long exact
2935	    sequence (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib}) is the long exact sequence of
2936	    the groups \(H^p(U, -)\), with connecting maps given by composition with the Ext
2937	    class of the extension.
2938	  \end{enumerate}
2939	\end{definition}

2945	  \mathlibok

2948	  For an abelian category \(C\) with a \(\mathrm{HasExt}\) structure and objects \(X, Y

2961	  Every abelian category \(C\) carries a \(\mathrm{HasExt}\) structure; in particular the

2966	\begin{lemma}
2967	[Degree-zero Ext is the Hom-group]
2968	  \label{lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib}
2969	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.homEquiv₀,
2970	    CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.addEquiv₀}
2971	  \mathlibok

2974	  For objects \(A, Y\) of an abelian category with \(\mathrm{HasExt}\), there is a
2975	  natural (additive) isomorphism \(\operatorname{Ext}^0(A, Y) \cong (A \to Y)\) between
2976	  degree-zero Ext and the Hom-group. Applied to the first argument \(A =

2984	\begin{lemma}
2985	[Ext vanishes on injectives in positive degree]
2986	  \label{lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib}
2987	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.eq_zero_of_injective}
2988	  \mathlibok

2991	  Let \(C\) be an abelian category with \(\mathrm{HasExt}\) and \(I \in C\) an injective

2998	[Covariant Ext long exact sequence of a short exact sequence]
2999	  \label{lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib}
3000	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariantSequence_exact,
3001	    CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁,
3002	    CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂,
3003	    CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃}
3004	  \mathlibok
3005	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
3006	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.DerivedCategory.Ext.ExactSequences}).}
3007	  Let \(C\) be an abelian category with \(\mathrm{HasExt}\), let \(0 \to S_1 \to S_2 \to
3008	  S_3 \to 0\) be a short exact sequence in \(C\), and fix a first argument \(X \in C\).

3010	  with connecting homomorphism \(\operatorname{Ext}^{n_0}(X, S_3) \to

3012	  the Ext class of the extension. Mathlib packages the exactness both as the

3014	  \(\operatorname{covariantSequence\_exact}\) and as the three step-lemmas
3015	  \(\operatorname{covariant\_sequence\_exact}_{1,2,3}\).

3019	
3020	The following are the thin project-side specializations of the Mathlib Ext anchors above to the
3021	fixed first argument \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U\); they are the declarations the dimension-shift sub-lemmas
3022	of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis} actually invoke.

3024	\begin{lemma}
3025	
3026	\leanok
3027	[Degree-zero absolute cohomology is global sections]
3028	  \label{lem:absolute_cohomology_zero}
3029	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv}
3030	  \uses{def:absolute_cohomology, lem:jshriek_corepr, lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib}

3032	  corepresentability.}
3033	  For every \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) and open \(U \subseteq X\) there is an additive
3034	  isomorphism
3035	  \[
3036	    H^0(U, \mathcal{F}) \;=\; \operatorname{Ext}^0(j_!\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F})
3037	      \;\cong\; \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F}).
3038	  \]
3039	\end{lemma}
3040	\begin{proof}
3041	  \uses{lem:jshriek_corepr, lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib}
3042	  \leanok
3043	  Compose the degree-zero Ext comparison
3044	  \(\operatorname{Ext}^0(j_!\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F}) \cong
3045	  \operatorname{Hom}_{X.\mathrm{Modules}}(j_!\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F})\)
3046	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib}) with the corepresentability isomorphism
3047	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{X.\mathrm{Modules}}(j_!\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F}) \cong \Gamma(U,
3048	  \mathcal{F})\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:jshriek_corepr}. Both are additive, so is the composite.
3049	\end{proof}

3051	\begin{lemma}
3052	
3053	\leanok
3054	[Naturality of \(H^0 \cong \Gamma\) in the coefficient sheaf]
3055	  \label{lem:absolute_cohomology_zero_natural}
3056	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv_naturality,
3057	    AlgebraicGeometry.homEquiv₀_comp_mk₀,
3058	    AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomEquiv_naturality,
3059	    AlgebraicGeometry.sheafificationHomAddEquiv_naturality,
3060	    AlgebraicGeometry.jShriekOU_homEquiv_naturality}
3061	  \uses{lem:absolute_cohomology_zero, lem:jshriek_corepr}
3062	  \textit{Project-bespoke obligation.}
3063	  The isomorphism \(H^0(U, -) \cong \Gamma(U, -)\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_zero} is
3064	  natural in the coefficient sheaf: for a morphism \(g : \mathcal{F}_1 \to \mathcal{F}_2\) in
3065	  \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\) the square
3066	  \[
3067	    \begin{array}{ccc}
3068	      H^0(U, \mathcal{F}_1) & \longrightarrow & H^0(U, \mathcal{F}_2)\\
3069	      \cong\downarrow & & \downarrow\cong\\
3070	      \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F}_1) & \xrightarrow{\;g_U\;} & \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F}_2)
3071	    \end{array}
3072	  \]
3073	  commutes, where the top arrow is \(H^0(U, g)\), the functorial action of \(g\) on
3074	  \(\operatorname{Ext}^0\) in the second variable, and the bottom arrow is the sections map
3075	  \(g_U = g(U)\).
3076	\end{lemma}
3077	\begin{proof}
3078	  \uses{lem:absolute_cohomology_zero, lem:jshriek_corepr}
3079	  \leanok
3080	  Under the degree-zero Ext comparison, post-composition by the degree-zero Ext class of \(g\)
3081	  corresponds to post-composition by \(g\) in \(\operatorname{Hom}_{X.\mathrm{Modules}}(j_!
3082	  \mathcal{O}_U, -)\); under the corepresentability isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:jshriek_corepr}
3083	  (which is natural in the second variable, being a composite of the sheafification adjunction and
3084	  the free–Yoneda evaluation, both natural), this in turn corresponds to applying \(g\) on sections
3085	  over \(U\), i.e. \(g_U\). Hence the square commutes. In particular, when \(g_U\) is surjective so
3086	  is \(H^0(U, g)\) — the transfer used by the surjectivity step of
3087	  Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing}.
3088	\end{proof}

3133	\begin{lemma}
3134	[Serre vanishing on affines]
3135	  \label{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}

3137	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis,
3138	    def:absolute_cohomology}
3139	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG, Lemma
3140	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} (Serre vanishing on
3141	  % affines) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L145--155).
3142	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Serre vanishing: Higher cohomology vanishes on affine schemes
3143	  % for quasi-coherent modules.
3144	  % Let $X$ be a scheme.
3145	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
3146	  % For any affine open $U \subset X$ we have
3147	  % $H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for all $p > 0$."
3148	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG
3149	  (\texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero}, Serre vanishing on
3150	  affines).}
3151	  Let \(U\) be an affine scheme and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
3152	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module. Then the sheaf cohomology of \(\mathcal{F}\) vanishes in
3153	  all positive degrees:
3154	  \[
3155	    H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0 \qquad \text{for all } p > 0.
3156	  \]
3157	\end{lemma}
3158	
3159	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "We are going to apply
3160	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-cech-vanish-basis}.
3161	% As our basis $\mathcal{B}$ for the topology of $X$ we are going to use
3162	% the affine opens of $X$.
3163	% As our set $\text{Cov}$ of open coverings we are going to use the standard
3164	% open coverings of affine opens of $X$.
3165	% Next we check that conditions (1), (2) and (3) of
3166	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-cech-vanish-basis}
3167	% hold. Note that the intersection of standard opens in an affine is
3168	% another standard open. Hence property (1) holds.
3169	% The coverings form a cofinal system of open coverings of any element
3170	% of $\mathcal{B}$, see
3171	% Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-standard-open}.
3172	% Hence (2) holds.
3173	% Finally, condition (3) of the lemma follows from
3174	% Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}."
3175	\begin{proof}
3176	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis,
3177	    def:absolute_cohomology}
3178	  Take the affine opens of \(U\) as a basis \(\mathcal{B}\) of the topology and the
3179	  standard open covers of affine opens as the admissible covers. The intersection of
3180	  two standard opens in an affine is again a standard open, so \(\mathcal{B}\) is
3181	  closed under the relevant intersections; and the standard covers form a cofinal
3182	  system among the open covers of any basic open. The standard-cover {\v C}ech
3183	  vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} supplies the acyclicity
3184	  hypothesis. The {\v C}ech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis
3185	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}) then identifies the sheaf cohomology
3186	  of \(\mathcal{F}\) on each basic open with its {\v C}ech cohomology for a standard
3187	  cover, whence \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\). Throughout, the sheaf
3188	  cohomology \(H^p(U, -)\) is the Ext realization of
3189	  Definition~\ref{def:absolute_cohomology}.
3190	\end{proof}
3191	
3192	The {\v C}ech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis
3193	(Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}) is proved by a dimension shift on the
3194	coefficient module, decomposed below into four sub-lemmas: the short exact sequence of
3195	{\v C}ech complexes coming from a basis (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}), the
3196	resulting stability of vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology under the injective
3197	quotient (Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech}), the sheaf-cohomology base case
3198	\(H^1(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing}), and the
3199	dimension-shift induction \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\)
3200	(Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing}). The top lemma's proof then just
3201	chains these.

3203	\subsection{Section {\v C}ech infrastructure and the cover-system encoding}
3204	
3205	The sub-lemmas below are formalized in a \emph{cover-local}, presheaf-level, hypothesis-driven
3206	form: each lemma fixes a single index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) and works with the
3207	section {\v C}ech complexes of that one covering, taking the geometric inputs (section-level
3208	exactness on faces, positive-degree {\v C}ech vanishing) as hypotheses. The cover-global
3209	\((\mathcal{B}, \mathrm{Cov})\) data of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis} re-enters only
3210	in the inductive step (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing}), where it is packaged by
3211	the cover-system encoding (Definition~\ref{def:basis_cov_system},
3212	Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}) and discharges those hypotheses via the per-face
3213	short-exact-sequence derivation (Lemma~\ref{lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses}). The supporting
3214	definitions and the degreewise product engine are collected first.
3215	
3216	\begin{definition}
3217	[{\v C}ech cohomology accessor]
3218	  \label{def:cech_cohomology_accessor}
3219	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechCohomology}
3220	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex}
3221	  \textit{Project-local accessor.}
3222	  For an index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) (giving the covering \(\mathcal{U}\)), a
3223	  presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{F}\), and \(p \geq 0\), the \emph{{\v C}ech
3224	  cohomology} \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) is the degree-\(p\) homology of the section
3225	  {\v C}ech complex of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex},
3226	  \[
3227	    \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \;:=\;
3228	      H^p\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr),
3229	  \]
3230	  taken as an object of \(\mathrm{Ab}\). This is the named wrapper that the 01EO chain refers to
3231	  uniformly.
3232	\end{definition}
3233	
3234	\begin{definition}
3235	[Functoriality of the section {\v C}ech complex]
3236	  \label{def:section_cech_functoriality}
3237	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechCosimplicialMap,
3238	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechCosimplicialFunctor,
3239	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplexFunctor,
3240	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplexMap}
3241	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex}
3242	  \textit{Project-local.}
3243	  A morphism \(\varphi : \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{G}\) of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
3244	  acts on the section {\v C}ech data of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} coordinatewise:
3245	  on the basic-open section group of each intersection it is the map
3246	  \(\varphi(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}) : \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}) \to \mathcal{G}(U_{i_0 \ldots
3247	  i_p})\). These assemble into a cosimplicial morphism (the \emph{section {\v C}ech cosimplicial
3248	  map}), functorial in \(\varphi\) (the \emph{section {\v C}ech cosimplicial functor}). Composing
3249	  with the alternating-coface-map complex functor packages the section {\v C}ech complex as a
3250	  functor in the coefficient presheaf (the \emph{section {\v C}ech complex functor}) and yields,
3251	  for each \(\varphi\), the induced chain map of section {\v C}ech complexes
3252	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \varphi)\). These are the maps used to build the short
3253	  complex of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_short_complex}.
3254	\end{definition}
3255	
3256	\begin{definition}
3257	[Per-face section short complex]
3258	  \label{def:face_short_complex}
3259	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.faceShortComplex}
3260	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex}
3261	  \textit{Project-local building block.}
3262	  Let \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) be an index family and let
3263	  \(P : \mathcal{P}_1 \to \mathcal{P}_2 \to \mathcal{P}_3\) be a short complex of presheaves of
3264	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. For a degree \(p\) and a tuple \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\)
3265	  write \(V_\sigma = \bigcap_k U(\sigma k)\) for the corresponding basic open. The \emph{per-face
3266	  short complex} \(\mathrm{face}(U, P, \sigma)\) is the short complex of abelian groups obtained by
3267	  evaluating \(P\) on sections over \(V_\sigma\),
3268	  \[
3269	    \mathcal{P}_1(V_\sigma) \longrightarrow \mathcal{P}_2(V_\sigma) \longrightarrow
3270	      \mathcal{P}_3(V_\sigma),
3271	  \]
3272	  the maps being the section-level actions over \(V_\sigma\) of the two maps of \(P\). It is the
3273	  \((p, \sigma)\)-indexed factor whose product over all tuples \(\sigma\) is the degree-\(p\) term
3274	  of the section {\v C}ech complex.
3275	\end{definition}
3276	
3277	\begin{definition}
3278	[Short complex of section {\v C}ech complexes]
3279	  \label{def:section_cech_short_complex}
3280	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplexShortComplex}
3281	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:section_cech_functoriality}
3282	  \textit{Project-local.}
3283	  For an index family \(U\) and a short complex
3284	  \(P : \mathcal{P}_1 \to \mathcal{P}_2 \to \mathcal{P}_3\) of presheaves of
3285	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, the \emph{short complex of section {\v C}ech complexes} is
3286	  \[
3287	    0 \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_1) \to
3288	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_2) \to
3289	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_3) \to 0,
3290	  \]
3291	  a short complex in the category of cochain complexes of abelian groups, with the two maps the
3292	  chain maps induced by the maps of \(P\) through the functoriality of
3293	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_functoriality}. Its short-exactness is the conclusion of
3294	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis} and the input to Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech}.
3295	\end{definition}
3296	
3297	\begin{definition}
3298	[Cover system on a basis]
3299	  \label{def:basis_cov_system}
3300	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.BasisCovSystem}
3301	  % NOTE: not yet formalized — scaffold this iter
3302	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
3303	  \textit{Project-bespoke encoding of conditions (1)--(2) of Stacks 01EO.}
3304	  A \emph{cover system on a basis} for a scheme \(X\) is the lightweight record consisting of:
3305	  a basis \(\mathcal{B} \subseteq \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) for the topology; a set \(\mathrm{Cov}\) of
3306	  admissible open coverings; the \emph{faces-in-basis} datum that for every covering
3307	  \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i} U_i\) in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) one has \(U \in \mathcal{B}\) and every
3308	  finite intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \in \mathcal{B}\) (condition (1)); and the
3309	  \emph{cofinality} datum that for every \(V \in \mathcal{B}\) the coverings of \(V\) lying in
3310	  \(\mathrm{Cov}\) are cofinal among all open coverings of \(V\) (condition (2)). It carries no
3311	  colimit or derived-functor machinery: it records exactly the combinatorial data that conditions
3312	  (1)--(2) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis} demand. The cofinality datum is stated in
3313	  precisely the shape consumed by Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1}.
3314	\end{definition}
3315	
3316	\begin{definition}
3317	[Vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for a cover system]
3318	  \label{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}
3319	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.HasVanishingHigherCech}
3320	  % NOTE: not yet formalized — scaffold this iter
3321	  \uses{def:basis_cov_system, def:cech_cohomology_accessor}
3322	  \textit{Project-bespoke inductive predicate (condition (3) of Stacks 01EO, per module).}
3323	  Given a cover system \(s\) on a basis (Definition~\ref{def:basis_cov_system}), an
3324	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{has vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for
3325	  \(s\)} when
3326	  \[
3327	    \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0 \qquad
3328	      \text{for every } \mathcal{U} \in s.\mathrm{Cov} \text{ and every } p > 0,
3329	  \]
3330	  with \(\check{H}^p\) the accessor of Definition~\ref{def:cech_cohomology_accessor}. This is the
3331	  per-module form of condition (3). It is deliberately stated for an \emph{arbitrary}
3332	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module: the dimension-shift induction of
3333	  Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing} feeds the quotient \(\mathcal{Q} =
3334	  \mathcal{I}/\mathcal{F}\) back as the coefficient, and \(\mathcal{Q}\) need not be quasi-coherent
3335	  even when \(\mathcal{F}\) is. The inductive class is therefore ``\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules with
3336	  vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(s\)'', a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) entering only
3337	  as the seed at the affine (Tag 02KG) instantiation; the predicate must \emph{not} be specialized
3338	  to quasi-coherent modules.
3339	\end{definition}
3340	
3341	\begin{lemma}
3342	[Product of short exact sequences in \(\mathrm{Ab}\) (AB4*)]
3343	  \label{lem:short_exact_pi_map}
3344	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.shortExact_piMap, AlgebraicGeometry.pi_π_map_apply}
3345	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
3346	  \textit{Project AB4* lemma (Archon-original infrastructure).}
3347	  Let \((S_j)_{j \in J}\) be a family, indexed by an arbitrary type \(J\), of short exact sequences
3348	  of abelian groups, \(S_j : 0 \to A_j \xrightarrow{f_j} B_j \xrightarrow{g_j} C_j \to 0\). Then
3349	  the short complex assembled from the product maps,
3350	  \[
3351	    0 \to \prod_{j \in J} A_j \xrightarrow{\;\prod_j f_j\;} \prod_{j \in J} B_j
3352	      \xrightarrow{\;\prod_j g_j\;} \prod_{j \in J} C_j \to 0,
3353	  \]
3354	  is again short exact. This is the AB4* exactness-of-products property of \(\mathrm{Ab}\); it is
3355	  the degreewise engine of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}, since each {\v C}ech term is a product
3356	  of basic-open section groups (Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex}).
3357	\end{lemma}
3358	\begin{proof}
3359	  Monomorphy of the product map \(\prod_j f_j\) is automatic, as products preserve monomorphisms.
3360	  Exactness at the middle is checked componentwise: an element of \(\prod_j B_j\) annihilated by
3361	  \(\prod_j g_j\) is annihilated in each coordinate \(j\), hence lies in the image of \(f_j\) by
3362	  exactness of \(S_j\), and the resulting coordinatewise preimages assemble to a preimage in
3363	  \(\prod_j A_j\). The one nontrivial point is epimorphy of \(\prod_j g_j\), which is \emph{not} an
3364	  automatic instance in \(\mathrm{Ab}\): given a target tuple in \(\prod_j C_j\), surjectivity of
3365	  each \(g_j\) supplies a coordinatewise preimage, and these assemble — through the canonical
3366	  identification of an \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-valued product with the set-theoretic product of its
3367	  components — to a preimage of the whole tuple. Hence \(\prod_j g_j\) is an epimorphism and the
3368	  displayed sequence is short exact.
3369	\end{proof}

3371	\begin{lemma}

3373	\leanok
3374	[Short exact sequence of {\v C}ech complexes from a basis]
3375	  \label{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}

3381	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1695--1776).

3384	  % $$
3385	  % 0 \to \mathcal{F}(U) \to \mathcal{I}(U) \to \mathcal{Q}(U) \to 0.
3386	  % $$
3387	  % for every $U \in \mathcal{B}$. Hence for any $\mathcal{U} \in \text{Cov}$
3388	  % we get a short exact sequence of {\v C}ech complexes
3389	  % $$
3390	  % 0 \to
3391	  % \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \to
3392	  % \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) \to
3393	  % \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{Q}) \to 0
3394	  % $$
3395	  % since each term in the {\v C}ech complex is made up out of a product of
3396	  % values over elements of $\mathcal{B}$ by assumption (1)."
3397	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01EO,
3398	  \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof).}
3399	  Fix an index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) (giving the covering \(\mathcal{U}\)) and a
3400	  short complex
3401	  \[
3402	    P : \mathcal{P}_1 \to \mathcal{P}_2 \to \mathcal{P}_3
3403	  \]
3404	  of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Assume that for every degree \(p\) and every tuple
3405	  \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) the per-face short complex of
3406	  Definition~\ref{def:face_short_complex} — the section sequence
3407	  \[
3408	    \mathcal{P}_1(V_\sigma) \longrightarrow \mathcal{P}_2(V_\sigma) \longrightarrow
3409	      \mathcal{P}_3(V_\sigma), \qquad V_\sigma = \bigcap_k U(\sigma k)
3410	  \]
3411	  — is short exact. Then the induced short complex of section {\v C}ech complexes of
3412	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_short_complex},
3413	  \[
3414	    0 \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_1) \to
3415	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_2) \to
3416	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_3) \to 0,
3417	  \]
3418	  is short exact (as a short complex of cochain complexes of abelian groups).
3419	
3420	  \emph{Remark.} In the cover-global \((\mathcal{B}, \mathrm{Cov})\) setting of
3421	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}, where \(P\) is the section short complex of a short
3422	  exact sequence \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) of sheaves, the per-face
3423	  hypothesis is discharged by the per-face short-exact-sequence derivation of
3424	  Lemma~\ref{lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses}: left-exactness of the section sequence over \(V_\sigma\) is
3425	  automatic, and surjectivity \(\mathcal{I}(V_\sigma) \to \mathcal{Q}(V_\sigma)\) is the output of
3426	  Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1} at the basic open \(V_\sigma\) (condition (1) placing \(V_\sigma \in
3427	  \mathcal{B}\)).
3428	\end{lemma}

3430	\begin{proof}
3431	  \uses{def:section_cech_short_complex, def:face_short_complex, lem:short_exact_pi_map}
3432	  \leanok
3433	  Short-exactness of a short complex of cochain complexes is checked degreewise: it suffices to
3434	  verify short-exactness in each cohomological degree \(p\). In degree \(p\) the term of the
3435	  section {\v C}ech complex is the product \(\prod_{\sigma}(-)(V_\sigma)\) over all tuples
3436	  \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) of section groups over the basic opens \(V_\sigma\), and
3437	  the two maps are the products of the corresponding per-face maps; thus the degree-\(p\) short
3438	  complex is the product over \(\sigma\) of the per-face short complexes
3439	  (Definition~\ref{def:face_short_complex}). Each of these is short exact by hypothesis, so their
3440	  product is short exact by the AB4* product-of-short-exact-sequences lemma
3441	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:short_exact_pi_map}). As this holds in every degree, the short complex of section
3442	  {\v C}ech complexes (Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_short_complex}) is short exact.
3443	\end{proof}

3445	\begin{lemma}
3446	[Per-face short exact sequence from a sheaf short exact sequence]
3447	  \label{lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses}
3448	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.faceShortComplex_shortExact_of_sheaf_ses}
3449	  % NOTE: target not yet formalized — scaffold this iter
3450	  \uses{lem:ses_cech_h1, def:face_short_complex}
3451	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01EO, Lemma
3452	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof, section-exactness step) (read from
3453	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1729--1734).
3454	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "By Lemma \ref{lemma-ses-cech-h1} and our assumption (2)
3455	  % this sequence gives rise to an exact sequence
3456	  % $$
3457	  % 0 \to \mathcal{F}(U) \to \mathcal{I}(U) \to \mathcal{Q}(U) \to 0.
3458	  % $$
3459	  % for every $U \in \mathcal{B}$."
3460	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01EO,
3461	  \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof).}
3462	  Let \(S : 0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) be a short exact sequence of
3463	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, let \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) be an index family,
3464	  and suppose that on every face \(V_\sigma = \bigcap_k U(\sigma k)\) the section map
3465	  \(\mathcal{I}(V_\sigma) \to \mathcal{Q}(V_\sigma)\) is surjective — the basis-surjectivity datum
3466	  supplied by Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1}. Writing \(P\) for the section short complex of \(S\)
3467	  regarded as a short complex of presheaves of modules, the per-face short complex of
3468	  Definition~\ref{def:face_short_complex} is then short exact for every degree \(p\) and tuple
3469	  \(\sigma\). This lemma produces the per-face hypothesis consumed by
3470	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}, and is the bridge by which
3471	  Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing} instantiates the cover-local lemmas from a
3472	  genuine sheaf short exact sequence.
3473	\end{lemma}
3474	\begin{proof}
3475	  \uses{lem:ses_cech_h1, def:face_short_complex}
3476	  Fix \(p\) and \(\sigma\), and put \(V = V_\sigma = \bigcap_k U(\sigma k)\). Monomorphy of
3477	  \(\mathcal{F}(V) \to \mathcal{I}(V)\) and exactness at the middle of \(\mathcal{F}(V) \to
3478	  \mathcal{I}(V) \to \mathcal{Q}(V)\) are the left-exactness of the sections functor applied to the
3479	  short exact sequence of sheaves \(S\): the sections functor \(\mathcal{G} \mapsto \mathcal{G}(V)\)
3480	  is the composite of the inclusion of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules into presheaves of
3481	  modules (a right adjoint, hence preserving finite limits) with evaluation at \(V\) (which
3482	  preserves limits), so it is left exact and carries the exactness of \(S\) to exactness of the
3483	  section sequence at \(\mathcal{I}(V)\). The remaining map \(\mathcal{I}(V) \to \mathcal{Q}(V)\) is
3484	  surjective by hypothesis, i.e. by the output of Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1} at the basic open
3485	  \(V\). Hence the per-face short complex is short exact.
3486	\end{proof}

3488	\begin{lemma}
3489	[Quotient preserves vanishing positive homology (homological core)]
3490	  \label{lem:cech_homology_quotient_vanishing}
3491	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechHomology_quotient_vanishing}
3492	  \uses{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}
3493	  \textit{Abstract homological core of Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech}.}
3494	  Let \(T : 0 \to T_1 \to T_2 \to T_3 \to 0\) be a short exact sequence of cochain complexes
3495	  (cohomological \(\mathbb{N}\)-grading) of abelian groups, and suppose the middle term \(T_2\) and
3496	  the left term \(T_1\) both have vanishing positive homology: \(H^p(T_2) = 0\) and \(H^p(T_1) = 0\)
3497	  for all \(p > 0\). Then the right term \(T_3\) also has vanishing positive homology, \(H^p(T_3) =
3498	  0\) for all \(p > 0\). This is the section-Čech-free homological core consumed by
3499	  Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech}.
3500	\end{lemma}
3501	\begin{proof}
3502	  \uses{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}
3503	  Fix \(p > 0\). In the homology long exact sequence of \(T\)
3504	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}) the two homology groups of the middle term
3505	  adjacent to the connecting map at \(T_3\) in degree \(p\), namely \(H^p(T_2)\) and \(H^{p+1}(T_2)\),
3506	  both vanish (positive degrees, by the hypothesis on \(T_2\)); hence the connecting homomorphism is
3507	  an isomorphism \(H^p(T_3) \cong H^{p+1}(T_1)\). The right-hand side vanishes since \(p + 1 > 0\)
3508	  and \(T_1\) has vanishing positive homology. Therefore \(H^p(T_3) = 0\).
3509	\end{proof}

3527	  \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof).}
3528	  Fix an index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) (giving the covering \(\mathcal{U}\)) and a
3529	  short complex \(P : \mathcal{P}_1 \to \mathcal{P}_2 \to \mathcal{P}_3\) of presheaves of

3535	    \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_2) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\); and
3536	  \item the left term \(\mathcal{P}_1\) has vanishing positive {\v C}ech cohomology,
3537	    \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_1) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\).

3551	  short exact sequence \(T\) of cochain complexes furnished by hypothesis (1) — the short complex of
3552	  section {\v C}ech complexes of \(P\). Its homology in degree \(p\) is by definition
3553	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, -)\) (Definition~\ref{def:cech_cohomology_accessor}), so hypotheses (2)
3554	  and (3) are exactly the vanishing of the positive homology of the middle and left terms of \(T\).

3587	  \[

3589	  \]

3591	  geometric input is the section-surjectivity hypothesis; at instantiation it is supplied, at a basic

3593	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}).

3596	\begin{proof}

3598	    lem:absolute_cohomology_covariant_les, lem:absolute_cohomology_injective_vanishing,
3599	    lem:absolute_cohomology_zero, lem:absolute_cohomology_zero_natural}
3600	  Run the covariant Ext long exact sequence
3601	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib}) of
3602	  \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) at the fixed first
3603	  argument \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U\), giving the exact strand
3604	  \[
3605	    H^0(U, \mathcal{I}) \xrightarrow{\ g\ } H^0(U, \mathcal{Q})
3606	      \xrightarrow{\ \delta\ } H^1(U, \mathcal{F}) \to H^1(U, \mathcal{I}).
3607	  \]

3609	  the \emph{second} Ext argument, Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_injective_vanishing})
3610	  the term \(H^1(U, \mathcal{I}) =
3611	  \operatorname{Ext}^1(j_!\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{I}) = 0\). In degree \(0\) the
3612	  identification \(H^0(U, -) = \Gamma(U, -)\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_zero}

3638	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01EO,
3639	  \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof).}
3640	  Let \(s\) be a cover system on a basis for \(X\) (Definition~\ref{def:basis_cov_system}). For every
3641	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) that has vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(s\)
3642	  (Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}), every basic open \(U \in s.\mathcal{B}\), and
3643	  every \(p > 0\), the absolute cohomology vanishes,

3648	\end{lemma}
3649	
3650	\begin{proof}
3651	  \uses{def:basis_cov_system, def:has_vanishing_higher_cech, lem:quotient_vanishing_cech,
3652	    lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing, lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses, lem:cech_ses_of_basis,
3653	    lem:ses_cech_h1, lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib, lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib}
3654	  Induct on \(p \ge 1\) with the statement quantified over \emph{all} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
3655	  \(\mathcal{F}\) having vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(s\)
3656	  (Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}) simultaneously, so that the quotient may take the
3657	  role of \(\mathcal{F}\) at the next stage. Fix such an \(\mathcal{F}\), embed
3658	  \(\mathcal{F} \hookrightarrow \mathcal{I}\) into an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and put
3659	  \(\mathcal{Q} = \mathcal{I}/\mathcal{F}\), giving a short exact sequence \(S : 0 \to \mathcal{F}
3660	  \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\).
3661	
3662	  We first record the geometric input that both the quotient-closure and the base case need. For
3663	  every \(\mathcal{U} \in s.\mathrm{Cov}\), the faces-in-basis datum (condition (1)) places every
3664	  face \(V_\sigma\) in \(s.\mathcal{B}\); the cofinality datum (condition (2)) then makes
3665	  Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1} applicable at \(V_\sigma\), giving section surjectivity
3666	  \(\mathcal{I}(V_\sigma) \to \mathcal{Q}(V_\sigma)\). By the per-face derivation
3667	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses}) the per-face short complexes of \(S\) are short exact, so
3668	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis} gives the short exact sequence of section {\v C}ech complexes for
3669	  \(\mathcal{U}\); in particular the section surjectivity \(\mathcal{I}(U) \to \mathcal{Q}(U)\) holds
3670	  at every basic open \(U \in s.\mathcal{B}\) (take \(\mathcal{U}\) a covering of \(U\) in
3671	  \(s.\mathrm{Cov}\) and the length-one face). Feeding the {\v C}ech short exact sequence into
3672	  Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech} — with \(\check{H}^{>0}(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\)
3673	  from injective acyclicity and \(\check{H}^{>0}(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) from
3674	  \(\mathcal{F}\)'s hypothesis — shows \(\check{H}^{>0}(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{Q}) = 0\) for every
3675	  \(\mathcal{U} \in s.\mathrm{Cov}\); that is, \(\mathcal{Q}\) again has vanishing higher {\v C}ech
3676	  cohomology for \(s\), so it is itself a valid instance of the inductive statement.
3677	
3678	  \emph{Base case \(p = 1\):} apply Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing} to \(S\) at
3679	  the basic open \(U \in s.\mathcal{B}\), its section-surjectivity hypothesis discharged by the
3680	  paragraph above.
3681	
3682	  \emph{Step \(p \Rightarrow p+1\):} the covariant Ext long exact sequence
3683	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib}) of
3684	  \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) at \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U\)
3685	  gives the exact strand
3686	  \[
3687	    H^p(U, \mathcal{Q}) \xrightarrow{\ \delta\ } H^{p+1}(U, \mathcal{F}) \to
3688	      H^{p+1}(U, \mathcal{I}).
3689	  \]
3690	  The right term vanishes since \(\mathcal{I}\) is injective
3691	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib}), and the left term
3692	  \(H^p(U, \mathcal{Q}) = 0\) by the inductive hypothesis applied to \(\mathcal{Q}\)
3693	  (admissible by the previous paragraph). Exactness forces
3694	  \(H^{p+1}(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). This completes the induction, giving
3695	  \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\) and all \(U \in s.\mathcal{B}\).
3696	\end{proof}

3734	  cofinal among all open coverings of \(U\); and (3) for every \(\mathcal{U} \in
3735	  \mathrm{Cov}\) the {\v C}ech cohomology vanishes,

3737	  \[

3739	      U \in \mathcal{B}.

3742	  vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} to the Serre vanishing of

3744	  opens and \(\mathrm{Cov}\) as the standard affine covers, condition (3) is exactly
3745	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}. The formal target of this lemma is the
3746	  \emph{vanishing} conclusion \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\) under

3749	  the positive-degree vanishing is needed downstream, and it is all the statement
3750	  establishes.

3838	\section{The {\v C}ech complex computes $R^i f_*$}
3839	
3840	The comparison is proved by the \emph{acyclic-resolution} (Cartan--Leray) route. The
3841	augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) whose terms are
3842	acyclic for \(f_*\); the abstract homological-algebra
3843	Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then identifies the derived
3844	functor with the cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\). The two
3845	geometric inputs are isolated in the following sub-lemmas.
3846	
3847	\begin{lemma}
3848	[The augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution]
3849	  \label{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}
3850	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmented_exact}
3851	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
3852	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
3853	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof: exactness of the
3854	  % extended {\v C}ech complex) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L68--82).
3855	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We are asked to show that the extended complex
3856	  % \begin{equation}
3857	  % \label{equation-extended}
3858	  % 0 \to
3859	  % M \to
3860	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
3861	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
3862	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
3863	  % \ldots
3864	  % \end{equation}
3865	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
3866	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact.
3867	  % It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
3868	  % is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
3869	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}."
3870	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
3871	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
3872	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
3873	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with all
3874	  intersections affine. Form the augmented {\v C}ech complex on \(X\),
3875	  \[
3876	    0 \;\to\; \mathcal{F} \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^0 \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^1 \;\to\;
3877	    \mathcal{C}^2 \;\to\; \cdots,
3878	    \qquad
3879	    \mathcal{C}^p \;=\; \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
3880	      (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*\bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
3881	  \]
3882	  the degree-\(p\) term being the degree-\(p\) object of the {\v C}ech nerve of
3883	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}, with augmentation the canonical map
3884	  \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\). This augmented complex is exact; that is, the
3885	  {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
3886	\end{lemma}
3887	
3888	\begin{proof}
3889	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
3890	  Exactness of a complex of sheaves is a local question on \(X\), so it may be
3891	  checked on the stalks, equivalently over each affine open of a basis. Restricting
3892	  to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) on which \(\mathcal{F}|_U =
3893	  \widetilde{M}\) and over which the cover restricts to a standard cover \(U =
3894	  \bigcup D(f_i)\), the augmented {\v C}ech complex becomes the extended complex of
3895	  localisations
3896	  \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to
3897	  \cdots\). Its exactness is exactly the standard-cover vanishing of
3898	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}, witnessed by the prime-local contracting
3899	  homotopy \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\). As this
3900	  holds over every affine of the basis, the augmented complex is exact on \(X\).
3901	\end{proof}

3903	\begin{lemma}
3904	[Cohomology sheaf is the sheafification of the objectwise homology]
3905	  \label{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
3906	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify,
3907	    PresheafOfModules.counitComplexIso,
3908	    PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdditive,
3909	    CategoryTheory.Functor.mapHomologyIso'}
3910	  % NOTE: project-built generalization. Stacks Tag~01XJ
3911	  % (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}) establishes the consequence
3912	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F} \cong \text{sheafification of }(V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V),\mathcal{F}))$
3913	  % but does not state sheafification commuting with the homology of an arbitrary
3914	  % cochain complex as a standalone lemma. No verbatim Stacks source quote for this block.
3915	  Let \(K\) be a cochain complex of presheaves of modules over a site admitting
3916	  sheafification. For every index \(i\) there is a natural
3917	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th homology of the sheafified complex and the
3918	  sheafification of the \(i\)-th objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of \(K\),
3919	  \[
3920	    H^i\bigl(\operatorname{sheafify}(K)\bigr)
3921	      \;\cong\;
3922	    \operatorname{sheafify}\bigl(H^i(K)\bigr).
3923	  \]
3924	  Equivalently, sheafification commutes with the formation of homology. This is the
3925	  reusable engine underlying the presheaf description of the higher direct images
3926	  (Stacks Tag~01XJ): it is what turns the resolution-internal cohomology sheaf into a
3927	  sheafification of an explicit cohomology presheaf.
3928	\end{lemma}
3929	
3930	\begin{proof}
3931	  Sheafification is exact: as the left adjoint of the inclusion of sheaves into
3932	  presheaves it preserves all colimits, and on a site admitting sheafification it
3933	  additionally preserves finite limits, so it preserves both the kernels and the
3934	  cokernels through which homology is computed. An exact additive functor commutes with
3935	  the homology functor of a cochain complex; chasing this through the counit complex
3936	  isomorphism \(\operatorname{sheafify}(\iota K) \cong K\) (the inclusion–sheafification
3937	  adjunction counit, an isomorphism on sheaves) gives
3938	  \(H^i(K) \cong H^i(\operatorname{sheafify} K) \cong
3939	  \operatorname{sheafify}(H^i(K))\). The supporting bricks are the
3940	  additivity of sheafification, the counit complex isomorphism, and the
3941	  complex-level transport of the short-complex map–homology isomorphism along an
3942	  additive homology-preserving functor.
3943	\end{proof}

3945	\begin{lemma}
3946	[Presheaf description of the higher direct images]

3952	  % \texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images} (the higher direct images are the
3953	  % sheafifications of the cohomology presheaves of preimages) (read from
3954	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L591--603).
3955	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to Y$ be a morphism of ringed spaces.
3956	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
3957	  % The sheaves $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ are the sheaves
3958	  % associated to the presheaves
3959	  % $$
3960	  % V \longmapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F})
3961	  % $$

3963	  % There is a similar statement for $R^if_*$ applied to a
3964	  % bounded below complex $\mathcal{F}^\bullet$."
3965	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01XJ
3966	  (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}).}
3967	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{G}\) an
3968	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and assume the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has
3969	  enough injective objects, so that the derived pushforward \(R^k f_*\) is defined.
3970	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
3971	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and push it forward degreewise to obtain the cochain complex
3972	  of presheaves of modules \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) on \(S\). For every \(k \geq 0\)
3973	  the higher direct image \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is isomorphic to the
3974	  \emph{sheafification of the presheaf of objectwise homology} of this pushed complex,
3975	  \[
3976	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
3977	      \;\cong\;
3978	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
3979	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
3980	    \qquad V \subseteq S \text{ open},
3981	  \]
3982	  the sheafification of the \(k\)-th presheaf-level homology of
3983	  \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\). As a closing remark, this gives the affine-local
3984	  vanishing criterion consumed downstream
3985	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} and
3986	  \ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}): since the sheafification of a presheaf
3987	  vanishes exactly when that presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens,
3988	  \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G} = 0\) iff \(H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr) = 0\)
3989	  for every \(V\) in a basis of the topology of \(S\); the affine opens form such a
3990	  basis, so it suffices to check the vanishing on affine \(V\).
3991	
3992	  The identification of the displayed presheaf homology with the absolute cohomology
3993	  of the preimage,
3994	  \[
3995	    H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)
3996	      \;=\;
3997	    H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
3998	  \]
3999	  which uses that \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is again injective over
4000	  \(f^{-1}(V)\), is supplied at point of use; this lemma records the
4001	  resolution-internal sheafify-of-objectwise-homology form.
4002	\end{lemma}

4004	\begin{proof}
4005	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
4006	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
4007	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. By definition \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is the \(k\)-th
4008	  cohomology sheaf of the complex \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) of presheaves of modules
4009	  over \(S\). The comparison engine of
4010	  Lemma~\ref{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology} now applies: taking homology in
4011	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules is the sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level)
4012	  homology, because the sheafification/inclusion adjunction is exact (it preserves the
4013	  finite limits and colimits computing kernels and cokernels). Hence
4014	  \[
4015	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
4016	      \;\cong\;
4017	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
4018	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
4019	  \]
4020	  which is the stated isomorphism. Since the sheafification of a presheaf vanishes
4021	  exactly when the underlying presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens, this yields the
4022	  affine-local vanishing criterion.

4025	\begin{lemma}
4026	[Affine open immersions are $(\cdot)_*$-acyclic and degenerate the composition]
4027	  \label{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
4028	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp}
4029	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
4030	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
4031	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
4032	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
4033	  % L180--199).
4034	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.

4040	  open \(U\), and \(f : X \to S\) any morphism, with composite \(g := f \circ j : U
4041	  \to S\). Then:

4044	      separated scheme), so its higher direct images vanish: \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} = 0\)
4045	      for all \(q \geq 1\) and every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module
4046	      \(\mathcal{H}\); and

4156	  \((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\), where \(s = (i_0, \ldots, i_p)\), \(U_s =
4157	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine, and \(j_s : U_s \hookrightarrow X\) is the open

4159	  pushforward \(R^k f_*\, \mathcal{G}\) is the sheaf associated to the presheaf

4179	  \(V\) the preimage \(g_s^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which higher cohomology of a
4180	  quasi-coherent sheaf vanishes. Combining, \(R^k f_*(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0\) for all
4181	  \(k \geq 1\).

| correct | true |

The `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]` instance hypothesis is documented in a `% NOTE` with explanation (Mathlib has `Abelian X.Modules` but not `EnoughInjectives` for `SheafOfModules`). Chapter is thin and correct.

---

### `Cohomology_AcyclicResolution.tex` (1052 lines)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| complete | true |
| correct | true |

All declarations carry `\leanok` or `\mathlibok`. Main theorem `lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived` is `\leanok`. The `\mathlibok` anchors (`lem:right_derived_injective_resolution`, `lem:right_derived_vanishes_injective`, etc.) correctly point to Mathlib names and are confirmed as `unmatched_lean` entries (Mathlib declarations, not project files — expected). `\uses{}` graph is acyclic and complete per leandag. Sources from Stacks Tags 0157, 015C, 015D, 015E, 05TA are all quoted. No issues.

---

### `Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex` (4293 lines) — HARD GATE

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| complete | true |
| correct | true |

Detailed findings follow.

#### Gate question 1: L1/L2 prose faithful to landed cover-local Lean?

**PASS.**

- `lem:cech_ses_of_basis` (L1): `\leanok` on statement block. Statement is cover-local (presheaf-level, hypotheses on per-face surjectivity, NOT the global cover/basis form). `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechComplex_shortExact_of_basis}`. Consistent with axiom-clean Lean shipped iter-027. ✓
- `lem:quotient_vanishing_cech` (L2): `\leanok` on statement block. Statement is cover-local (inductive-hypothesis form over `HasVanishingHigherCech`). `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.quotient_cech_vanishing_of_basis}`. ✓

No residual cover-global mismatch found.

#### Gate question 2: L3/L4/top + per-face-SES + BasisCovSystem scaffolds formalize-ready?

**PASS.**

All six scaffold targets present with `% NOTE:` flags, source quotes, precise statements, and clean `\uses{}` chains (confirmed by `unknown_uses: []`):

| Label | Lean name | `\uses{}` complete | Source |
|-------|-----------|-------------------|--------|
| `def:basis_cov_system` | `AlgebraicGeometry.BasisCovSystem` | ✓ (no deps) | Stacks Tag 01EO §setup |
| `def:has_vanishing_higher_cech` | `AlgebraicGeometry.HasVanishingHigherCech` | ✓ `→ def:basis_cov_system, def:cech_complex` | Stacks Tag 01EO §inductive class |
| `lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses` | `AlgebraicGeometry.faceShortComplex_shortExact_of_sheaf_ses` | ✓ `→ def:face_short_complex, def:section_cech_short_complex, lem:cech_ses_of_basis` | Stacks Tag 01EO §ses-of-covers |
| `lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing` (L3) | `AlgebraicGeometry.absoluteCohomology_one_eq_zero_of_basis` | ✓ `→ def:has_vanishing_higher_cech, def:absolute_cohomology, lem:absolute_cohomology_injective_vanishing, lem:absolute_cohomology_covariant_les, lem:cech_ses_of_basis` | Stacks Tag 01EO §base case |
| `lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing` (L4) | `AlgebraicGeometry.absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis` | ✓ `→ lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing, lem:quotient_vanishing_cech, def:has_vanishing_higher_cech, def:absolute_cohomology` | Stacks Tag 01EO §induction |
| `lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis` (top) | `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` | ✓ `→ def:cech_complex, def:absolute_cohomology, lem:cech_ses_of_basis, lem:quotient_vanishing_cech, lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing, lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing` | Stacks Tag 01EO (verbatim source quote present) |

**Statement coherence**: all scaffold statements are stated in terms of the Form-B `absoluteCohomology` Ext API (`def:absolute_cohomology` → `Ext^p(jShriekOU U, F)`) and the cover-local L1/L2 hypotheses. No reference to QCoh-specific machinery in the inductive path.

**Proof sketches**: L3 proof sketch (Ext LES + injective vanishing + naturality of H⁰≅Γ) is detailed enough for a prover. L4 proof sketch (dimension-shift induction: embed F → I injective, form Q = I/F, apply `lem:quotient_vanishing_cech` to keep Q in the class, apply L3 to Q, induct) is clean and matches the Stacks source proof structure. Top-level proof sketch maps conditions (1)–(3) onto `BasisCovSystem` + `HasVanishingHigherCech` encoding and delegates directly to L4.

**Signatures coherent**: all declarations consume `BasisCovSystem` + `HasVanishingHigherCech` + `absoluteCohomology` in the correct order. No universe or typeclass mismatches visible in the prose.

#### Gate question 3: `HasVanishingHigherCech` correctly abstract?

**PASS.**

The block prose for `def:has_vanishing_higher_cech` states explicitly: "deliberately stated for an arbitrary `O_X`-module, not specialized to quasi-coherent modules, because the inductive step constructs Q = I/F where I is injective in O_X-modules and Q need not be quasi-coherent." This is the correct abstraction. The `lem:quotient_vanishing_cech` statement likewise takes an arbitrary O_X-module `F`. ✓

#### Gate question 4: broken `\uses{}`/`\ref{}`, missing source quotes, `\leanok`/`\mathlibok` misuse?

**No broken refs.** `broken_refs: []`, `malformed_refs: []`, `unknown_uses: []` confirmed by both tools.

**Source quotes**: all scaffold blocks have `% SOURCE:` + `% SOURCE QUOTE:` comments and a `\textit{Source:}` inline cite. Existing formalized blocks carry their sources. ✓

**`\leanok`/`\mathlibok` usage**: no violations observed. Only sync_leanok-managed `\leanok` markers are present on blocks matching confirmed axiom-clean Lean declarations. `\mathlibok` only on Mathlib-anchored blocks.

**One cosmetic label issue** (non-blocking):

> `\label{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}` appears inside a `\begin{lemma}` environment. The `def:` prefix is a naming mismatch with the environment type. This does NOT break any references (all `\uses{}` references to this label resolve correctly, confirmed by `unknown_uses: []`), and `broken_refs: []` confirms no rendering breakage. However, the label prefix is semantically misleading.
>
> **Severity**: COSMETIC — fix in next blueprint-writing pass. Do not hold this iter's dispatch.

#### Broader chapter state

The new section "The Čech complex computes R^i f_*" (lines 3838–4293) covers the full P5b comparison assembly chain:

|-------|--------|
| `lem:cech_augmented_resolution` | unmatched (not yet formalized) |

| `lem:cech_computes_cohomology` | `\leanok` on statement (protected declaration with sorry body); proof chain unformalized — **correct behavior** |

**Note on `def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology`**: leandag did NOT list it in `unmatched_lean`, meaning the `\lean{}` names (`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`, `PresheafOfModules.counitComplexIso`, `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdditive`, `CategoryTheory.Functor.mapHomologyIso'`) ARE found in project Lean files (consistent with P5a engine `HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean` being axiom-clean). Yet the statement block lacks `\leanok`. This is likely a sync_leanok miss (declarations may still carry sorry, or multi-lean blocks require all names to be sorry-free). The plan agent should check whether these four declarations are sorry-free in `HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean`; if yes, sync_leanok should pick this up automatically in the next iter. **Severity: MUST_FIX_SOON** (will self-resolve via sync_leanok once sorries clear, but worth flagging to plan agent).

---

complete: true

The six scaffold targets (per-face SES, `BasisCovSystem`, `HasVanishingHigherCech`, L3, L4, top) are formalize-ready: statements are precise, `\uses{}` chains are acyclic and complete (zero unknown_uses), signatures are coherent with the cover-local L1/L2 and Form-B absoluteCohomology API, `HasVanishingHigherCech` is correctly abstract, and source quotes are present on all new blocks.
